title: Name Tradeoffs Explicitly
guidance: A good implementation plan states what is gained lost and deferred instead of hiding behind generic best practices
do: Record tradeoffs and why the chosen path is acceptable
dont: Do not propose abstraction-heavy plans without acknowledging cost

title: Prefer Reversible Phases
guidance: Phases should be scoped so the team can stop or roll back without rewriting the whole approach
do: Break work into reversible checkpoints with acceptance criteria
dont: Do not build plans that only succeed if every later phase also succeeds

title: Every Plan Needs Phases Dependencies and Risks
guidance: Planning output should include steps dependencies acceptance criteria and explicit risks
do: Deliver a phased sequence with mitigations and handoffs
dont: Do not hand back a loose idea list without sequencing
